DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Thomas J. LaLanne; Law Office of Thomas J. LaLanne; 415-434-1122; appearing on behalf of plaintiffs and cross-defendants Tony Garnicki, Ocean Avenue Real Estate Fund, LLC, and Sanchez RE, LLC.
Ann McFarland Draper; Draper Law Offices; 415-989-5620; appearing on behalf of plaintiffs and cross-defendants Tony Garnicki, Ocean Avenue Real Estate Fund, LLC, and Sanchez RE, LLC.
Glenn C. Kelble; Sweeney & Kelble APC; 310-955-4050; appearing on behalf of defendants Anchor Loans, LP, JRM Financial, LLC, and Superior Default Services, Inc.

Both sides contest the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Draper requests 60 days leave to amend. The court grant's plaintiff's request. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. Special Demurrer: Defendants' demurrer on the grounds of Capacity to Sue, Tender, and Uncertainty is overruled. With respect to Lack of Standing as to Garnicki and Ocean LLC, the demurrer is overruled; the demurrer as to Sanchez RE is sustained with leave to amend. (Bostanian v. Liberty Savings Bank (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 1075, 1084.) A cause of action accruing after a bankruptcy petition is filed is property of the bankruptcy estate. The bankruptcy estate is comprised of property including: all legal or equitable interests of the debtor in property as of the commencement of the case (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(1)), and [a]ny interest in property that the estate acquires after the commencement of the case. (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(7).) [P]roperty includes causes of action. (United States v.Whiting Pools, Inc. (1983) 462 U.S. 198, 205, fn. 9.) Here, Sanchez RE's claims accrued on April 21, 2020 when the foreclosure sales occurred on the Corbett and Mississippi properties which was after the February 17, 2020 filing of the Chapter 7 petition. Accordingly, their claims are property of the bankruptcy estate. (11 United States Code Sec. 541(a)(7).)
